Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First let me understand the standard Hire to Retire process 1 Post Job Ad 2 Screen Candidates 3 Conduct Interviews 4 Make Hiring Decision 5 Onboard Employee 6 Add to Payroll 7 Close Case This is typically a sequential process with some possible variations

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now lets analyze Model 1 Nodes Post Screen Decide Interview Onboard Payroll Close Edges Post Screen Screen Decide Screen Interview Decide Onboard Onboard Payroll Payroll Close The order constraints are Post Screen Screen Decide Screen Interview Decide Onboard Onboard Payroll Payroll Close

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: There is NO constraint that Interview Decide Decide can happen before Interview or after or concurrently This means you can make a hiring decision before conducting interviews or concurrently major anomaly

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Interview has no outgoing edges so Interview is a sink no constraint Interview Close so Close can happen before Interview Thats very wrong So Model 1 anomalies Interview and Decide unordered Decision can be made before interviews Interview no prerequisite for subsequent activity severe anomaly

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now Model 2 Nodes Post Screen Interview Decide loop onboarding xor payroll Close Edges Post Screen Post Interview Interview Decide Decide loop onboarding loop onboarding xor payroll xor payroll Close loop onboarding LOOP Onboard skip allows repetition of Onboard xor payroll XOR Payroll skip payroll optional

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Screen has no outgoing edges Screen disconnected from main flow can happen after Close Screen and Interview unordered can interview before screening moderate anomaly Payroll optional significant anomaly Onboarding loop unusual

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: Let me re read Model 1 carefully Screen Decide Screen Interview Decide Onboard etc So after Screen both Decide and Interview can happen in any order

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: Wait both models have a dangling activity Model 1 Interview dangling Model 2 Screen dangling

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Comparing Model 1 main flow Post Screen Decide Onboard Payroll Close missing Interview before Decide Model 2 main flow Post Interview Decide Onboard optional Payroll Close missing Screen before Interview

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: In Model 1 valid trace Post Screen Decide Onboard Payroll Close Interview Close before Interview In Model 2 Post Interview Decide Onboard Payroll Close Screen Screen after Close

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Which closer Model 2 preserves Interview before Decide crucial Model 1 allows Decide before Interview fundamental violation Model 2 anomalies less severe screening order optional payroll loop minor

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Model 2 closer because preserves essential causal link Interview Decide core hiring logic Model 1 breaks decision making integrity
